description: city and county seat of Clallam County, Washington State, United States
description_wiki: Port Angeles ( AN-jəl-əs) is a city in and the county seat of Clallam County, Washington, United States.

description: unincorporated community in Clallam County, Washington
description_wiki: La Push is a small unincorporated community situated at the mouth of the Quillayute River in the Western Olympic Peninsula.

description: 4th-century BCE Greek philosopher
description_wiki: Plato ( PLAY-toe; Greek: Πλάτων, Plátōn; born c. 428–423 BC, died 348/347 BC) was an ancient Greek philosopher of Classical Athens who is most commonly considered the foundational thinker of the Western philosophical tradition.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

entity: West Coast Pulp and Paper Workers Union
surface_forms: West Coast Pulp and Paper Workers Union

description: 1970 incursion into eastern Cambodia by US and South Vietnamese forces
capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: park and plaza in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: Mount Vernon Square is a city square and neighborhood in the Northwest quadrant of Washington, D.C. The square is located where the following streets would otherwise intersect: Massachusetts Avenue NW, New York Avenue NW, K Street NW, and 8th Street NW.

description: reserve force of the United States Army and Air Force
description_wiki: The National Guard is a military reserve organization of the United States Department of Defense (DoD).

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

description: Christian denominational family (1652-)
public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States
description_wiki: The University of Washington (UW, and informally U-Dub or U Dub) is a public research university in Seattle, Washington, United States.

surface_forms: the company that I worked in
description: American global aerospace and defense corporation
description_wiki: The Boeing Company ( BO-ing) is an American multinational corporation that designs and manufactures airplanes, rotorcraft, rockets, satellites, and missiles worldwide.

description: NASA and ESA space telescope (launched 1990)
description_wiki: The Hubble Space Telescope (HST or Hubble) is a space telescope that was launched into low Earth orbit in 1990 and remains in operation.

description: country in East Asia
description_wiki: Mongolia is a landlocked country in the East Asia region.

description: sound along the northwestern coast of the U.S. state of Washington
description_wiki: Puget Sound ( PEW-jit; Lushootseed: x̌ʷəlč IPA: [ˈχʷəlt͡ʃ] WHULCH) is a complex estuarine system of interconnected marine waterways and basins located on the northwest coast of the U.S. state of Washington.

description: large rainforest in South America
description_wiki: The Amazon rainforest, also called the Amazon jungle or Amazonia, is a moist broadleaf tropical rainforest in the Amazon biome that covers most of the Amazon basin of South America.

description: largest city of the state of Louisiana, United States
description_wiki: New Orleans (commonly known as NOLA or  The Big Easy, among other nicknames) is a consolidated city-parish located along the Mississippi River in the U.S. state of Louisiana.

description: street in New Orleans, Louisiana, USA
description_wiki: Canal Street (French: Rue du Canal) is a major thoroughfare in the city of New Orleans.

description: American singer, actor and civil rights activist (1927–2023)
description_wiki: Harry Belafonte  ( BEL-ə-FON-tee; born Harold George Bellanfanti Jr.; March 1, 1927 – April 25, 2023) was an American singer, actor, and civil rights activist who popularized calypso music with international audiences in the 1950s and 1960s.

description: American folk music group popular in the 1960s
description_wiki: Peter, Paul and Mary was an American folk group formed in New York City in 1961 during the American folk music revival.

description: meeting place of the United States Congress
description_wiki: The United States Capitol, often called the Capitol or the Capitol Building, is the seat of the United States Congress, the legislative branch of the federal government.

entity: Society of Professional Engineering Employees in Aerospace
surface_forms: Society of Professional Engineering Employees in Aerospace
description_wiki: The Society of Professional Engineering Employees in Aerospace (SPEEA), IFPTE Local 2001 is a professional labor union representing more than 24,000 engineers, technical workers and other professionals in the aerospace industry.

description: 1967-1997 aerospace manufacturer and defense contractor
description_wiki: McDonnell Douglas Corporation was a major American aerospace manufacturing corporation and defense contractor.
